name: Text sizing enforcement — tests that no text node can truncate
source: Root cause of real truncation failures in brainstorm output

input.userMessage: Plan a dashboard with a sidebar nav (6 items), page title with subtitle, 3 stat cards with labels and values, a data table with 5 columns, and a user profile section showing name, role, and email

expectedBehaviors: EVERY text node in the plan JSON has a sizing property
Body text and titles use { width: fill, height: hug }
Short labels (nav items, button text) use { width: hug, height: hug }
Sidebar nav items use Button (Tertiary gray), not raw text
User profile uses Avatar label group component
Stat card labels and values have explicit sizing
Table column headers and cell text have fill sizing
Plan JSON passes the text sizing validation check with zero warnings
antiPatterns: ANY text node missing a sizing property
Text node with content longer than 20 chars using { width: hug } (will clip)
Nav items as plain text frames (should be Button components)
User profile as text + ellipse (should be Avatar label group)
Stat values with no parent width constraint

evaluationCriteria.textSizingCoverage: Is sizing present on 100% of text nodes? Not 90%, not 95% — 100%
evaluationCriteria.sizingAppropriatenessn: Are the sizing values correct? Fill for body, hug for labels?
evaluationCriteria.validationPassRate: Would the plan pass Step 6 text sizing validation with zero warnings?
evaluationCriteria.componentMapping: Are mandatory patterns (nav, avatar, alerts) using library components?
